title: Syracuse’s STEAM school opens with welcomes and field trips on historic first day: ‘You all are pioneers’
summary: Syracuse opened STEAM High School on September 3, 2025, a regional magnet school in a renovated 122-year-old downtown building that represents a seven-year effort by local and state leadership. The inaugural class of 200+ ninth graders can specialize in nine STEAM-focused areas including data analytics, robotics, semiconductor manufacturing, and performing arts, with the school expected to grow to ~1,000 students. The $100M project received $71M in state investment and support from Micron Technology, positioning Syracuse to align education with regional workforce demands.

political_signal.description: Mayor Walsh championed this seven-year initiative (launched 2019) as flagship regional education-workforce development aligned with emerging tech sector (Micron semiconductor investment). Opening positions city as innovation hub.
